zh.lifeSummary: 當Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad於1426年出生在瑞典斯卡拉波格吞時，她的父親Anders Eriksson Ekeblad27歲，母親Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link22歲。她與Hans Jåpsson在瑞典達拉納卡爾波恩結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1546年在瑞典達拉納森德波恩享年120歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad于1426年出生在瑞典斯卡拉波格吞时，她的父亲Anders Eriksson Ekeblad27岁，母亲Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link22岁。她与Hans Jåpsson在瑞典達拉納卡爾波恩结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1546年在瑞典達拉納森德波恩享年120岁。

nameMeanings.name: Andersson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bjorn, Lars, Mats, Lennart, Nils, Per, Anders, Berndt, Hokan, Olle, Sten.  German Hans, Kurt, Alfons, Ernst, Gerd, Monika.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Anders, a vernacular form of Andreas .
Americanized form of Norwegian, North German, and very rare Danish patronymic Anderssen, a cognate of 1 above. Compare Andersen and Anderson .

variants.name: Andreas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Heinz, Otto, Beate, Bernd, Ernst, Ilse, Kurt.

German and English: from the New Testament Greek name Andreas, which gave rise to Andrew in English and vernacular derivatives in almost every other European language. Etymologically, it is from Greek andreios ‘manly’, an adjective derivative of anēr (genitive andros) ‘man, male’. This is a Greek translation of an unknown Aramaic original; it was the name of the first of Christ's disciples, the patron saint of both Scotland and Russia. The Scottish surname Anderson is far more common than its Russian equivalent, Andreev. The personal name was popular throughout Europe in various vernacular forms (including Italian Andrea, French André, Spanish Andrés, Scottish, North German, and Scandinavian Anders, Dutch Andries, Hungarian András, Czech Ondřej, Polish Andrzej and Jędrzej, and Russian Andrey).
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Andrejaš: from an old vernacular form of the personal name Andraž (from Latin Andreas), which is a variant of Andrej; see Andrew .

nameMeanings.name: Ekeblad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements ek ‘oak’ +blad ‘leaf’.

nameMeanings.name: Brita
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Borrowing of the Irish name Brighid ( see Bridget ). This name owes its enormous popularity in Scandinavia, especially in Sweden, to St Birgitta ( 1304–73 ), patron saint of Sweden. She was a noblewoman who bore her husband eight children. After his death, she founded an order of nuns, the ‘Bridgettines’ or Order of the Most Holy Saviour. She also went to Rome, where she attempted to reform religious life.

variants.name: Birgitte
meanings: Borrowing of the Irish name Brighid ( see Bridget ). This name owes its enormous popularity in Scandinavia, especially in Sweden, to St Birgitta ( 1304–73 ), patron saint of Sweden. She was a noblewoman who bore her husband eight children. After his death, she founded an order of nuns, the ‘Bridgettines’ or Order of the Most Holy Saviour. She also went to Rome, where she attempted to reform religious life.

She was a noblewoman who bore her husband eight children. After his death, she founded an order of nuns, the ‘Bridgettines’ or Order of the Most Holy Saviour. variants.name: Britt
meanings: contracted form of Birgit ( see Bridget ), made famous in the English-speaking world by the Swedish actress Britt Ekland ( b. 1942 ; her surname was originally Eklund ).

variants.name: Britta
meanings: Borrowing of the Irish name Brighid ( see Bridget ). This name owes its enormous popularity in Scandinavia, especially in Sweden, to St Birgitta ( 1304–73 ), patron saint of Sweden. She was a noblewoman who bore her husband eight children. After his death, she founded an order of nuns, the ‘Bridgettines’ or Order of the Most Holy Saviour. She also went to Rome, where she attempted to reform religious life.

meanings: Borrowing of the Irish name Brighid ( see Bridget ). This name owes its enormous popularity in Scandinavia, especially in Sweden, to St Birgitta ( 1304–73 ), patron saint of Sweden. She was a noblewoman who bore her husband eight children. After his death, she founded an order of nuns, the ‘Bridgettines’ or Order of the Most Holy Saviour. She also went to Rome, where she attempted to reform religious life.

nameMeanings.name: Beata
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Late Latin feminine form of Beatus ‘blessed’. The name was borne by an early Christian saint martyred in North Africa. Beata is widely used among Roman Catholics in Germany, Poland, and elsewhere; it is less common in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: The Engelbrekt Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Engelbrekt Engelbrektsson, a Swedish mine owner and statesman, led a rebellion against Eric of Pomerania, King of the Kalmar Union. Sweden was upset with the Danes' interference with Swedish exports and unjust collection of taxes. The Engelbrekt Rebellion led to the deposing of Eric of Pomerania and the eventual dissolution of the Kalmar Union.

historicEvents.title: The Russo-Swedish War
historicEvents.description: While in an alliance with Grand Prince Ivan III of Moscow, King Hans of Denmark and Norway invaded Sweden and was declared King of Sweden.

historicEvents.title: The Stockholm Bloodbath
historicEvents.description: A mass execution of Swedish nobles by Danish king Christian II is known as the Stockholm Bloodbath. Christian II invaded Sweden in 1519 and took hold of Stockholm in September 1520. Over 80 nobles were executed for heresy on November 8 and 9. The outrage from the executions led to the Swedish removal of support of the Kalmar Union.

historicEvents.title: Swedish War of Liberation
historicEvents.description: The Swedish War of Liberation was a civil war led by nobleman Gustav Vasa. As Christian II heard of the rebellion, he confronted Vasa's men, who won the battle at Brunbacks Ferry. Christian II was defeated by the Swedes in Stockholm and a truce was agreed upon. Vasa was given as a hostage as a show of good faith until the treaty was signed. The Danish King did not keep his word and Vasa was held as a prisoner for two years until his escape. A bounty was placed on Vasa's head. Vasa worked to inspire many to join his cause and lived under disguise until the Danes surrendered in 1525.

en.lifeSummary: When Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad was born in 1426, in Tun, Skaraborg, Sweden, her father, Anders Eriksson Ekeblad, was 27 and her mother, Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, was 22. She married Hans Jåpsson in Karlborn, Dalarna, Sweden. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1546, in Sundborn, Dalarna, Sweden, at the age of 120.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad est née en 1426, à Tun, Skaraborg, Suède, son père, Anders Eriksson Ekeblad, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Hans Jåpsson à Karlborn, Dalarna, Suède. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1546, à Sundborn, Dalarna, Suède, à l’âge de 120 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad 1426 in Tun, Skaraborg, Schweden geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Anders Eriksson Ekeblad 27 und ihre Mutter Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link 22. Sie hat in Karlborn, Dalarna, Schweden Hans Jåpsson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1546 im Alter von 120 Jahren in Sundborn, Dalarna, Schweden.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad è nata il nel 1426 nel Tun, Skaraborg, Svezia, sua padre, Anders Eriksson Ekeblad, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Hans Jåpsson nel Karlborn, Dalarna, Svezia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1546, nel Sundborn, Dalarna, Svezia, all'età di 120 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekebladが1426年にスウェーデンスカラボルグトゥンで生まれた時，父Anders Eriksson Ekebladは27歳，母Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Linkは22歳であった。スウェーデンダラーナカルルボルンでHans Jåpssonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1546年にスウェーデンダラーナスンドボルンで120歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad(이)가 1426년 스웨덴 스카라볼그 툰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Anders Eriksson Ekeblad(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 스웨덴 다라른아 칼본에서 Hans Jåpsson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1546년 스웨덴 다라른아 선드본에서 120세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad nasceu em 1426, em Tun, Skaraborg, Suécia, seu pai, Anders Eriksson Ekeblad, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Hans Jåpsson em Karlborn, Dalarna, Suécia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1546, em Sundborn, Dalarna, Suécia, com 120 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad родилась в 1426 г. в местности тунь, скараборг, Швеция, её отцу по имени Anders Eriksson Ekeblad было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hans Jåpsson в местности карлборн, даларна, Швеция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1546 г. в местности сундборн, даларна, Швеция в возрасте 120 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Brita Beata Piijk Andersson Ekeblad nació en 1426, en Tun, Skaraborg, Suecia, su papá, Anders Eriksson Ekeblad, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Ingeborg Märkvärdsdotter Link, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Hans Jåpsson en Karlborn, Dalarna, Suecia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1546, en Sundborn, Dalarna, Suecia, a la edad de 120 años.
